candidates.name: contour_angularity_index
candidates.construct: Curvilinear vs angular contour character of the interior: sharp angular contours elicit threat-related response and lower liking; curvilinear interiors are judged more beautiful and elicit approach. The most-replicated form-affect finding proposed to the panel, converged on independently by four seats.
literature: Bar & Neta 2006 (Psychological Science): curved objects preferred over matched sharp-angled ones
Bar & Neta 2007 (Neuropsychologia): sharp contours elevate amygdala activation (threat interpretation)
Vartanian et al. 2013 (PNAS): curvilinear architectural interiors judged more beautiful, engage ACC, predict approach decisions
Dazkir & Read 2012 (Environment and Behavior): curvilinear furniture settings elicit higher pleasure/relaxation
Chuquichambi et al. 2022 (meta-analysis; venue hedged): curvature preference reliable across stimulus types and cultures, moderate effect
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic OpenCV. Canny edges -> linked contour chains above minimum length -> Douglas-Peucker/turning-angle analysis with local circle fits. Decompose arclength into straight / smooth-arc / sharp-corner segments. Outputs: curve fraction (curved length / total contour length, 0-1) and corner density (sharp angles per unit contour length) as a separate threat subscore; optional eye-level-band weighting. Feeds the existing fluency-hedonics layer as a signed valence input. Base variant needs no segmentation; the person-masked variant rides segmentation and drops to AMBER.
candidates.rationale: Four independent seats proposed the same construct+computation; multi-lab, meta-analytically supported evidence; fully deterministic; measures contour SHAPE, orthogonal to existing edge clarity (sharpness) and fractal D (complexity).
candidates.collision_with_existing: Edge clarity/sharpness measures edge definition; fractal D measures scaling complexity — neither carries the curved-vs-angular sign. Genuinely new valence-bearing shape statistic.

candidates.name: spectral_discomfort_deviation
candidates.construct: Visual discomfort / visual stress from deviation of the image's Fourier amplitude spectrum from natural 1/f statistics, especially excess mid-spatial-frequency energy (Wilkins pattern-glare line); slope also indexes naturalness of spatial statistics.
literature: Field 1987 (JOSA A): natural scenes have ~1/f amplitude spectra
Fernandez & Wilkins 2008 (Perception): discomfort predicted by deviation from 1/f, excess energy at medium spatial frequencies
Juricevic, Land, Wilkins & Webster 2010 (Perception): discomfort tracks departure from natural image statistics
Penacchio & Wilkins 2015 (Vision Research): computational model — discomfort rises with CSF-weighted departure of Fourier energy from the natural 1/f profile
Graham & Field 2007; Redies et al. 2007 (Spatial Vision): artworks share natural-scene spectral statistics (naturalness side)
candidates.computation: Tier A, pure numpy/FFT, sandbox-verified by the proposing seat (recovered slope -1.95 on synthetic 1/f^2 noise, target -2.0). Grayscale -> Hann window -> 2D FFT -> radially averaged power spectrum over >=4 octaves -> OLS log-log slope alpha (with R^2). Outputs: (a) naturalness = closeness of alpha to natural value; (b) discomfort deviation = CSF-weighted positive mid-band residual per Penacchio-Wilkins; (c) orientation-resolved stripe-peak detector for high-contrast periodic slats/louvers (from the Oliva-seat variant). Cycles/degree calibration rests on a DECLARED FOV/viewing-distance assumption emitted with the score.
candidates.rationale: Four-seat convergence; the single best-replicated statistics->discomfort mechanism in the literature with an explicit published computational model; deterministic, model-free; complements glare risk (luminance) with pattern-induced discomfort.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Fractal D (box-counting on binarized edges) is correlated but misses spectral anomalies (stripes/grids) that keep edge density normal; glare risk is luminance-based. Fourier-domain complement, not a duplicate.

candidates.name: visible_vegetation_fraction
candidates.construct: Visible live vegetation in the occupant's actual view (plants, green walls, foliage through openings) — the core Stress Recovery Theory / Attention Restoration predictor: unthreatening natural content drives parasympathetic recovery and directed-attention restoration.
literature: Ulrich 1984 (Science): tree-view patients recovered faster post-surgery than wall-view patients
Ulrich et al. 1991 (J. Environmental Psychology): faster physiological stress recovery during natural vs urban scene exposure
Nieuwenhuis, Knight, Postmes & Haslam 2014 (JEP: Applied): green offices ~15% productivity gain over lean offices in field experiments
Bringslimark, Hartig & Patil 2009 (J. Environmental Psychology, review): modest but recurrent benefits; heterogeneous effect sizes support an uncertainty band
candidates.computation: Tier A, classical CV. (1) White-balance sanity check (gray-world deviation); ABSTAIN on grayscale/heavy cast. (2) HSV foliage-hue mask (green band, S/V thresholds) INTERSECT high local texture (Laplacian variance / local fractal-D above floor — excludes flat green paint and fabric). (3) Morphological cleanup. Outputs: pixel fraction 0-1, blob count, largest-blob share (one big plant vs distributed greenery), confidence flag; ABSTAIN when hue fires but texture test is ambiguous. Declared proxy: 'visible plausibly-living green material', not botanically verified.
candidates.rationale: Three-seat convergence on the strongest construct link in the whole proposal set (Ulrich 1984 canonical); cheap deterministic computation; AMBER honestly retained for the heuristic vegetation classifier.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C19 restoration/nature-contact is Tier-B plan ADJACENCY (can you reach nature); this is photographic evidence of nature visible from the viewpoint — catches the desk-facing-blank-wall inversion C19 cannot see.

candidates.name: mystery_occlusivity
candidates.construct: Mystery — the promise of more information upon moving deeper (Kaplans' strongest preference predictor), formalized as Benedikt's isovist occlusivity: the fraction of the visible-field boundary that is an occluding edge to hidden, reachable space rather than a solid surface.
literature: Kaplan & Kaplan 1989 (The Experience of Nature): mystery the most consistent positive predictor in the preference-matrix studies
Benedikt 1979 (Environment and Planning B): occlusivity — summed length of occluding radial isovist edges — as the formal measure of hidden adjacent space
Franz & Wiener 2008 (Environment and Planning B): isovist measures incl. occlusivity correlate with reported spatial experience indoors
Stamps 2004 (meta-analytic): mystery-preference relation positive but heterogeneous — attribute ships with an uncertainty flag, not a strong valence claim
Herzog & Miller 1998 (hedged): mystery raises perceived danger in low-legibility settings — motivates the intelligibility gate
candidates.computation: Tier B on the inferred plan grid. Cast 360 rays from the camera cell (optionally seat cells) -> isovist polygon; classify boundary segments SOLID vs OCCLUDING (radial jump discontinuity with walkable cells beyond). occlusivity = occluding length / isovist perimeter; flood-fill hidden-adjacent walkable area through occluding edges for mystery = occlusivity * log(1+hidden_area), room-normalized. Emit uncertainty flag when C3 intelligibility is low (mystery-danger decoupling); optional Tier-A depth-map corroborator (half-occluded deep floor regions, from the materials-seat variant). ABSTAIN below plan-inference confidence threshold.
candidates.rationale: Three-seat convergence; canonical formalization of the top Kaplan predictor; honest Stamps-heterogeneity flag and intelligibility gating preserved. AMBER because wholly downstream of the heuristic photo-to-plan chain.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Prospect measures how FAR you see; C11 measures seat-level protection. Neither measures promised-but-hidden space — occlusivity is the complement of prospect at the isovist boundary.

candidates.name: window_view_layer_content
candidates.construct: Window view quality via view CONTENT — number of visible environmental layers (sky / landscape / ground) and vegetation presence in the view — a validated driver of emotion, restoration, and cognitive performance, distinct from daylight quantity or plan-level view access; codified in EN 17037.
literature: Ko, Kent, Schiavon, Levitt & Betti 2022 (LEUKOS): view-quality framework — content (layer count), access, clarity
Markus 1967 (Building Science): views containing all three layers preferred over truncated views
Ko, Schiavon et al. 2020 (Building and Environment): window view improved emotion and working-memory performance vs no-view at matched conditions
Ulrich 1984 (Science) and Kaplan 2001 (Environment and Behavior): view CONTENT (nature) carried the outcome effects
EN 17037:2018: normative anchor scoring views by layers visible
candidates.computation: Tier A. (1) Detect glazing: high-luminance quadrilateral regions on segmented wall planes (depth-discontinuity / sky-chroma cross-checks); rectify by homography. (2) If aperture interior is near-uniformly clipped (LDR blowout), window presence stays SCORED but content = UNKNOWN — designed-in abstention, never guessed. (3) Else deterministic layer rules: sky (upper, high Y, low texture, blue/neutral), vegetation (excess-green + mid texture, reusing V3's detector), ground/built (remainder). Outputs: layers_present in {0..3} mapped per Ko/EN 17037, greenery fraction, aperture solid-angle fraction. ABSTAIN if no window detected on any wall plane.
candidates.rationale: Four-seat convergence; standards-grade (EN 17037) operationalization of the variable that actually carried Ulrich's and Kaplan's effects; exemplary abstention design for the common blown-out-window case. AMBER: rides plane segmentation and heuristic window detection.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C9 view equity and C10 daylight proximity are plan ACCESS metrics; vertical illuminance and glare treat windows as light sources. Nothing existing scores WHAT the glazing shows.

candidates.name: subband_entropy_clutter
candidates.construct: Visual clutter as encoding inefficiency: Shannon entropy of steerable-pyramid subband coefficients over luminance and chrominance — how incompressible the scene is per glance; the efficient-coding substrate of processing load and (dis)fluency.
literature: Rosenholtz, Li & Nakano 2007 (Journal of Vision): Subband Entropy predicts clutter judgments and search performance; explicit efficient-coding account
Portilla & Simoncelli 2000 (IJCV): joint wavelet statistics as the currency of early visual texture encoding
Henderson, Chanceaux & Smith 2009 (Journal of Vision): clutter measures of this family predict search in real-world scenes
Reber, Schwarz & Winkielman 2004: fluency->affect bridge (proxy-grade, flagged; inherited from the existing hedonics layer)
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic. CIELab; steerable pyramid (3 scales x 4 orientations) on L plus coarse chroma decompositions; per-subband fixed-bin coefficient histograms -> Shannon entropy; luminance-dominant weighted sum with weights taken from the published code, not invented. Output: bits + corpus-percentile 0-1, plus per-tile map for comparability with the legacy tiled proxy. Affect mapping inherits the existing inverted-U fluency machinery.
candidates.rationale: Two-seat convergence on the field-standard, behaviorally validated clutter measure; deterministic published algorithm; stress-tests the ad hoc legacy proxy by reporting agreement with it.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Sharper, citable companion to the existing ad hoc tiled clutter proxy and orthogonal to palette entropy (spatially blind color histogram); declared as validation-grade replacement, which is why it survives the no-duplication rule.

candidates.name: feature_congestion_clutter
candidates.construct: Visual clutter as local feature-space congestion: how 'full' the local distribution of color, contrast, and orientation already is — sets the salience threshold any sign, exit, or waving colleague must exceed to be noticed; directly predicts visual search cost.
literature: Rosenholtz, Li, Mansfield & Jin 2005 (CHI): Feature Congestion as covariance-ellipsoid clutter, validated on subjective judgments
Rosenholtz, Li & Nakano 2007 (Journal of Vision): predicts search RT better than object counts; clutter as effective set-size surrogate
Henderson, Chanceaux & Smith 2009 (Journal of Vision): transfers to real-world scene photographs
Beck, Lohrenz & Trafton 2010 (JEP: Applied): local clutter around targets matters — motivates the spatial map output
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic. CIELab; 3-level Gaussian pyramid; per level: color clutter = sqrt(det local covariance of (a*,b*)), contrast clutter = local std of band-passed L contrast, orientation clutter = ellipse volume of local oriented-energy covariance (4-orientation DoG bank). Minkowski pooling with published luminance-dominant weights, max over levels. Outputs: per-pixel clutter map + scalar, raw and corpus-percentile 0-1.
candidates.rationale: THE lab-validated search-cost predictor with interpretable units and a spatial map usable to audit C4 decision points for local congestion; deterministic; complements V6 (same units family, different aspect).
candidates.collision_with_existing: Existing tiled clutter proxy is ad hoc and palette entropy is global; feature congestion is covariance-based, locally normalized, and behaviorally anchored — declared sharper variant, not duplicate.

candidates.name: spectral_naturalness
candidates.construct: Spatial-envelope naturalness via orientation isotropy: man-made scenes concentrate spectral energy at cardinal (0/90 deg) orientations, natural content is isotropic — the best-validated bottom-up statistic of perceived naturalness, upstream of restoration/preference effects.
literature: Oliva & Torralba 2001 (IJCV): naturalness as the primary spatial-envelope axis, separable by orientation/spectral signatures
Torralba & Oliva 2003 (Network): natural vs man-made categories separable spectrally
Kardan et al. 2015 (Frontiers in Psychology): perceived naturalness and preference substantially predicted by low-level edge/orientation/color statistics
Berman et al. 2008 (Psych. Science); Ulrich 1984 (Science): downstream naturalness->restoration link, flagged as correlational support only
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic filtering. (a) Shannon entropy of the Gabor-energy orientation histogram (isotropy); (b) cardinal dominance ratio = energy(0/90 +/-15 deg)/total; (c) mid-band spectral shape; (d) optional weak green-hue co-signal with capped weight. Fixed weighted combination along the published discriminant direction, 0-1; also computable per-block yielding a naturalness map locating where natural content sits (window vs plants vs materials).
candidates.rationale: Pure classical filtering with a directly measured statistics->perceived-naturalness link; fills the gap that fractal D (rectilinear tile walls can be fractal) and warm/cool ratio (color proxy) leave open; complements V3 (pixel vegetation) with a structural naturalness signal.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C19 is plan-level; fractal D and warm/cool are different statistics. Orientation isotropy is the missing bottom-up naturalness axis.

candidates.name: fractal_mid_d_band_score
candidates.construct: Fractal fluency: proximity of the scene's fractal dimension to the empirically preferred and stress-reducing mid-D band (~1.3-1.5), plus coverage of the visual field in that band — the response-curve scoring the existing D outputs currently lack.
literature: Spehar, Clifford, Newell & Taylor 2003 (Computers & Graphics): preference peaks at D ~1.3-1.5 across fractal types
Hagerhall, Purcell & Taylor 2004 (J. Environmental Psychology): landscape-silhouette D predicts preference, peak ~1.3
Taylor 2006 (Leonardo) and Hagerhall et al. 2008 (Perception): mid-D viewing reduces physiological stress; frontal alpha maximal at mid-D
Abboushi, Elzeyadi, Taylor & Sereno 2019 (J. Environmental Psychology): mid-D fractal light patterns preferred in real interiors
Bies et al. 2016: systematic individual differences — the peak is a population mode, carried as a caveat
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic numpy on EXISTING intermediates (global box-count D and local-D tile field). (1) Fixed asymmetric trapezoid response kernel peaked on [1.3,1.5] with declared falloff constants; (2) band coverage = fraction of local-D tiles in [1.25,1.55]; (3) low-confidence flag when box-count fit R^2 < 0.98 (broken scaling; see V30). Output (band_score, coverage) with individual-differences caveat in metadata.
candidates.rationale: Near-zero implementation cost (rides existing outputs), direct multi-method literature (preference + skin conductance + qEEG + real-interior replication), and it converts an existing descriptive attribute into a scored one.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Uses existing fractal D but adds the literature-anchored response curve and coverage statistic the hedonics layer currently lacks for fractals specifically.

candidates.name: landmark_differentiation
candidates.construct: Lynch's 'singularity': a salient element functions as a landmark only if unique among co-visible candidates — high salience with low uniqueness (four identical red columns) gives conspicuity without reference value.
literature: Lynch 1960 (The Image of the City): landmark quality = singularity, contrast-with-context plus uniqueness
Sorrows & Hirtle 1999 (COSIT): visual salience of landmarks requires contrast AND distinctiveness
Raubal & Winter 2002 (GIScience): landmark suitability operationalized as measurable deviation of a candidate from its neighbors — the formal template followed here
candidates.computation: Tier A, fully classical. Take the existing landmark-salience module's top-k (<=6) candidate regions; per candidate build deterministic descriptors (Lab histogram, LBP texture histogram, Hu moments, area); differentiation of candidate i = min over j!=i descriptor distance (chi-square + z-normalized moment distance); attribute = differentiation of the most salient candidate, 0-1, plus repeated-element flag when >=2 candidates nearly coincide (colonnade condition). ABSTAIN with <2 candidates. Scope declared: within-frame uniqueness only, not building-wide.
candidates.rationale: Directly upgrades an existing attribute with the component the wayfinding literature identifies as what makes salience USABLE; classical pipeline; honest within-frame scoping.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Existing landmark salience measures figure-ground conspicuity; this measures uniqueness among the salient candidates — the missing Lynch component, explicitly complementary.

candidates.name: route_angular_continuity
candidates.construct: Dalton's 'follow your nose' principle: junctions offering near-straight continuations are cognitively cheaper and preferentially chosen; cumulative angular deviation predicts wayfinding difficulty better than topological turn counts.
literature: Conroy Dalton 2003 (Environment and Behavior): VR wayfinders select paths minimizing angular deviation and conserving linearity
Hillier & Iida 2005 (COSIT): least-angle segment measures predict pedestrian movement better than metric or topological distance
Turner 2001 (Space Syntax Symposium): angular segment-graph analysis formalism
candidates.computation: Tier B. Skeletonize walkable space to a centerline segment graph (medial-axis thinning); at each degree>=3 node compute minimum angular deviation from each incoming to any outgoing segment. Outputs: (1) fraction of decision nodes offering a continuation within 15 deg ('nose-followable junctions'); (2) mean cumulative angular deviation along entrance-to-deepest-cell shortest paths, length-normalized, inverted to [0,1]. ABSTAIN with <2 decision nodes.
candidates.rationale: Strong behavioral evidence (Dalton; Hillier-Iida movement prediction) for a variable C1-C4 genuinely lack: angular geometry of continuations. AMBER only because the plan grid is inferred; on a real plan this is GREEN-grade.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C1-C3 are visibility/topological; C4 counts decision points. None encodes angular deviation, the variable shown to drive route choice.

candidates.name: natural_material_fraction
candidates.construct: Material Connection with Nature (biophilic Pattern 9): visible-surface fraction of natural materials (wood, stone), with wood coverage following a dose-response (inverted-U, ~45% optimum) relation to comfort and autonomic relaxation.
literature: Tsunetsugu, Miyazaki & Sato 2007: 0/45/90% wood-coverage rooms — ~45% most comfortable; non-monotone autonomic responses
Nyrud & Bringslimark 2010 (review): interior wood associated with positive psychological responses, moderated by coverage; evidence base limited
Fell 2010 (UBC dissertation): wood office lowered skin-conductance sympathetic activation vs matched non-wood office
Browning, Ryan & Clancy 2014 (14 Patterns): Pattern-9 framing only (theory-grade)
candidates.computation: Tier A over segmented planes. WOOD = wood-band hue + oriented grain (structure-tensor coherence / Gabor anisotropy with single dominant orientation); STONE = low saturation, granular mid-frequency texture, no dominant orientation; reuse existing material-table labels where present. Outputs: wood_fraction, stone_fraction, natural_material_fraction (area-weighted, 0-1); wellbeing mapping via inverted-U centered ~0.45 wood coverage, mirroring the existing fluency machinery. Declared limit: cannot distinguish real wood from wood-print laminate — bounded stated proxy error since the cited responses were visually elicited.
candidates.rationale: Two-seat convergence; rare literature-anchored dose-response SHAPE, not just a direction; small-N physiological base honestly carried. AMBER: heuristic material classification.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Material table currently feeds only acoustic absorption; warm/cool is chromatic only. Re-aggregates material evidence toward a distinct biophilic-wellbeing construct.

candidates.name: edge_orientation_entropy
candidates.construct: First- and second-order entropy of edge orientations — diversity and spatial independence of oriented structure; a directly published positive predictor of aesthetic liking for man-made images, and the diversity axis opposing symmetry/coherence.
literature: Grebenkina et al. 2018 (Frontiers, Redies lab): first/second-order edge-orientation entropy predicted liking across man-made image categories
Redies, Brachmann & Wagemans 2017: traditional artworks show near-maximal edge-orientation entropy
Berlyne 1971: arousal-potential framing motivating raw-statistic output with response shape left to the hedonics layer
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic. Sobel gradients above fixed relative magnitude threshold; (a) first-order: Shannon entropy of magnitude-weighted 16-bin orientation histogram, normalized; (b) second-order: entropy of the joint orientation-pair distribution over fixed pixel-distance bins (Redies-lab method) capturing spatial independence. Both 0-1, fed raw to the existing hedonics layer (monotone vs inverted-U left to that layer).
candidates.rationale: The one fluency statistic proposed with a direct published preference result on man-made images; deterministic; complements horizontal symmetry and V32 coherence by capturing the opposing diversity axis.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Edge clarity measures gradient STRENGTH, palette entropy measures color; orientation distributional structure is unmeasured.

candidates.name: luminance_histogram_shape
candidates.construct: Visual comfort/preference predicted by the SHAPE of the luminance distribution (skew, kurtosis, percentile ratios, bright-tail structure), not just dispersion — the Wymelenberg/Inanici finding that luminance-distribution metrics beat illuminance metrics.
literature: Van Den Wymelenberg & Inanici 2014 (LEUKOS): luminance percentiles/ratios over the field of view predicted comfort and preference better than illuminance metrics; bounded non-uniformity often preferred
Van Den Wymelenberg & Inanici 2016 (LEUKOS, hedged): percentile/ratio metrics among strongest single preference predictors in larger pool
Amundadottir, Rockcastle, Sarey Khanie & Andersen 2017 (Building and Environment): distribution structure, not mean/variance, is the perceptually operative variable
candidates.computation: Tier A, photo only. Linearize to pseudo-luminance, work in log-Y: skewness, excess kurtosis, P95/P5, P75/P25, clipped-pixel fraction. Comfort-band score 0-1 (moderate spread + mild positive skew high; gloom and heavy bright-tail low, per tolerance-bound directions). Uncertainty flag at >2% clipped; ABSTAIN on comfort score above ~10% clipped (LDR censors the operative tail), raw stats still reported.
candidates.rationale: Directly extends existing brightness variance (2nd moment only) with the 3rd/4th moments and ratios the comfort literature validated; AMBER honestly retained because validation used HDR imagery and an 8-bit photo is an ordinal proxy.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Brightness variance: equal-variance scenes can have opposite skew/tail structure and opposite comfort predictions — declared complement.

candidates.name: spatial_contrast_composition
candidates.construct: Visual interest from the spatial ARRANGEMENT of light and dark — neighbor-difference spatial contrast plus the structure of shadowed regions (coherent deep shadow vs scattered darkness), distinct from any histogram statistic.
literature: Rockcastle & Andersen 2014 (Building and Environment): 'spatial contrast' metrics built precisely to separate daylight compositions identical global statistics cannot
Rockcastle et al. ~2015-2017 (with Andersen, years hedged): spatial contrast correlated with rated visual interest/excitement of daylit scenes
Amundadottir et al. 2017: gaze drawn to high-contrast daylight structure
candidates.computation: Tier A, photo only, no segmentation. Pseudo-luminance downsampled to fixed 64x64 grid; spatial_contrast = mean summed absolute 8-neighbor luminance difference / scene mean (single-frame adaptation of Rockcastle-Andersen). Dark mask (Y < 0.2 median): dark fraction, connected-component count/size distribution -> shadow_coherence. Outputs: spatial_contrast, dark_fraction, shadow_coherence, all deterministic pixel arithmetic.
candidates.rationale: Fixes the exact blindness of brightness variance (shuffle-invariance) and adds the stack's first darkness/shadow-structure attribute; AMBER because validation used HDR renderings under controlled viewing.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Brightness variance is spatially blind; glare risk sees only bright patches. First composition-of-contrast and shadow-structure measure.

candidates.name: sun_patch_geometry
candidates.construct: Interest/excitement elicited by the geometry of direct-sunlight patches (fragmentation, shape irregularity, coverage) on interior surfaces — the Chamilothori facade-perforation finding; same pixels as glare, opposite valence, different statistics.
literature: Chamilothori, Wienold & Andersen 2019 (Building and Environment): irregular/fragmented sunlight patterns rated more exciting and interesting in validated VR daylit scenes; physiological arousal differed by geometry
Chamilothori, Wienold & Andersen 2019 (LEUKOS): VR-vs-real-space perceptual validation
Chamilothori et al. early 2020s (LEUKOS, hedged): cross-regional replication of the interest response
candidates.computation: Tier A + plane segmentation. Linearize to pseudo-luminance; candidate sun pixels = above max(95th percentile, 0.85 Ymax) on floor/wall planes; keep components with hard shadow edges (high boundary gradient) to exclude diffusely bright surfaces. Outputs: patch area fraction, count, mean shape complexity P^2/(4πA), fragmentation index; composite interest 0-1. No hard-edged patch -> geometry sub-scores ABSTAIN (area scores 0); >2% clipped pixels -> LDR-censoring flag.
candidates.rationale: Well-replicated, ecologically validated construct filling a genuine gap (positive valence of light pattern); careful abstention design. AMBER: plane segmentation + LDR proxy photometry.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Glare risk flags bright patches as discomfort hazard; this computes perimeter-complexity/fragmentation of the same regions as an interest driver — orthogonal statistics, opposite valence.

candidates.name: popout_margin
candidates.construct: Preattentive conspicuity as signal-to-clutter: whether any element is a Mahalanobis outlier from its local feature distribution by enough SDs to pop out without serial search — predicts parallel vs serial finding of exits, signs, hazards.
literature: Rosenholtz 1999 (Vision Research): statistical saliency — popout when target is many SDs from the local distractor distribution
Duncan & Humphreys 1989 (Psychological Review): search efficiency governed by target-distractor similarity relative to distractor heterogeneity — the covariance-normalized distance computed here
Rosenholtz, Li & Nakano 2007: Feature Congestion is explicitly this ratio's denominator
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic, commensurate with V7 (same filter bank). Per-pixel feature f = (L*,a*,b*, 4-orientation DoG energies); Gaussian-weighted local mean/covariance over ~1.5-deg windows (declared FOV constant); Mahalanobis D(x) with Tikhonov-regularized covariance. popout_margin = mean(top 0.5% of D) − median(D), in SD units, plus location of maximum; reported at 2 window sizes if the FOV constant is disputed.
candidates.rationale: Detection-free, covariance-normalized complement to landmark salience; paired with V7 gives an honest signal-to-noise account (cluttered-but-signed vs calm-but-featureless). Deterministic.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Landmark salience scores detected candidates' distinctiveness; this measures the margin over local clutter in feature-congestion units, object-free.

candidates.name: vantage_prospect_refuge_balance
candidates.construct: Directional prospect-refuge at the camera vantage: deep sightlines ahead combined with close backing behind (seeing without being seen) — Appleton's construct operationalized front-vs-rear, needing no seat map.
literature: Appleton 1975 (The Experience of Landscape): prospect + back-protection preferred and read as safer
Dawes & Ostwald 2014 (and later book, hedged ~2018): prospect-refuge computed for real interiors from exactly these isovist max/min radial and view-behind measures
Stamps 2005 (Environment and Behavior): perceived safety varies systematically with boundary enclosure/permeability
candidates.computation: Tier B. Split 360 isovist radials by the recovered camera view axis into FRONT/REAR hemifields; prospect_f = median front radial; refuge_r = 1/(1+median rear radial); balance = (front−rear)/(front+rear) in [−1,1] (+1 = deep view with backed rear). ABSTAIN if vanishing-point view-axis recovery fails.
candidates.rationale: Adds the defining DIRECTIONAL structure of Appleton's construct at zero extra input, where C11 requires a seat map; clean deterministic geometry. AMBER: inferred plan + VP chain.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C11 is seat-level and needs a seat map; prospect is depth-only; enclosure is omnidirectional. Front/behind asymmetry is new.

candidates.name: boundary_permeability_split
candidates.construct: Visual vs locomotive permeability of the room boundary (see-through vs walk-through), which Stamps showed drive DIFFERENT judgments: enclosure/spaciousness tracks visual permeability, perceived safety tracks locomotive permeability.
literature: Stamps 2005 (Environment and Planning B, 'Isovists, enclosure, and permeability theory'): formal enclosure model from boundary permeability and view depth
Stamps 2005 (Environment and Behavior): experimental dissociation — spaciousness follows visual permeability, safety follows locomotive permeability
candidates.computation: Tier A+B, each channel abstaining independently. Tier A: visual_permeability = (glazed+open wall area)/(total wall area) over segmented wall planes, using the stack's existing brightness/depth-discontinuity machinery. Tier B: locomotive_permeability = fraction of isovist perimeter whose occluding edges open onto walkable floor-level cells (threshold width per unit boundary). Both 0-1.
candidates.rationale: Experimentally dissociated two-channel structure that the existing enclosure index provably conflates (glass box vs doorway-riddled masonry); enables a WB safety predicate plus a COG spaciousness predicate.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Existing enclosure index collapses both channels into one solid-angle number — declared refinement with cited dissociation evidence.

candidates.name: decision_point_preview_depth
candidates.construct: Pre-commitment visual information at junctions: how far down each branch a walker can see before committing (Weisman's visual access localized to choice points); blind junctions produce wrong turns even in high-integration plans.
literature: Weisman 1981 (Environment and Behavior): visual access one of four legibility factors predicting disorientation
Wiener, Hoelscher, Buechner & Konieczny 2012 (Psychological Research): gaze and choice biased toward options with longest sightlines at choice points
Braaksma & Cook 1980: origin-destination mutual visibility index — precedent for deterministic visibility scoring
Peponis, Zimring & Choi 1990 (Environment and Behavior): visually connected layouts channel search
candidates.computation: Tier B. Decision nodes (degree>=3) on the skeletonized centerline graph; from each node cast a +/-10-deg ray bundle down each branch; preview depth = free-space depth to first obstruction; per node take the MINIMUM option preview (worst-informed choice); attribute = mean of per-node minima normalized by plan diameter, plus per-node table. ABSTAIN with no decision nodes.
candidates.rationale: Per-branch per-junction minimum is genuinely absent from C1 (global average) and prospect (camera-only); direct behavioral gaze/choice evidence. AMBER: inferred grid.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C1 integration is a global mean; prospect is the camera vantage. A plan can have high mean integration yet blind junctions.

candidates.name: ceiling_height_percept
candidates.construct: Apparent vertical ceiling extent — independently drives openness, awe/approach, and abstract-vs-concrete processing style, dissociable from lateral enclosure (Vartanian factorial; Meyers-Levy priming).
literature: Vartanian et al. 2013 (PNAS): ceiling height crossed factorially with contour and openness as an independent driver of interior experience
Meyers-Levy & Zhu 2007 (J. Consumer Research): high ceilings prime relational/abstract processing, low ceilings item-specific/concrete
Vartanian et al. ~2015 (venue hedged): verticality/openness associated with approach and appeal
candidates.computation: Tier A. Line-segment detection + RANSAC vanishing points (Manhattan assumption) recover camera pitch; segment ceiling plane; estimate vertical visual angle of the floor-to-ceiling wall near image center and normalize by recovered room width -> apparent height-to-width ratio (~0.2 oppressive to ~1.5 lofty). ABSTAIN when no ceiling plane or VP recoverable.
candidates.rationale: Factorial experimental evidence plus a cognitive-style effect; isolates the vertical dimension that enclosure (lateral) and prospect (depth) both miss. AMBER: rides VP/Manhattan geometry.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Enclosure index = lateral boundedness; prospect = view depth; neither isolates verticality.

candidates.name: light_directionality_modelling
candidates.construct: Flow of light (Cuttle vector/scalar concept): strongly directional (hard, dramatic) vs fully diffuse (flat, dull) vs the moderate range that models faces and objects pleasantly — the first WHERE-does-light-come-from attribute.
literature: Cuttle 1971 (Lighting Research and Technology): vector/scalar illuminance ratio as the correlate of perceived flow of light and modelling
Cuttle 2008 (Lighting by Design): moderate vector/scalar ratios (~1.2-1.8, hedged) judged pleasant/natural for faces
Flynn, Spencer, Martyniuk & Hendrick 1973 (J. IES): lighting mode (diffuse vs directional) reliably shifts pleasantness, relaxation, clarity impressions
candidates.computation: Tier A + plane segmentation. Fit Y ~ a + b·u + c·v on large matte wall/floor regions in in-plane coordinates (existing VP homography); directionality = ||(b,c)||/mean Y, coherence = R^2. Penumbra proxy: gradient transition width along dark-mask boundaries (narrow = hard source). Area-weighted directionality 0-1 mapped through an inverted-U modelling-quality score (peak at moderate, per Cuttle), consistent with the existing fluency machinery. ABSTAIN without a sufficiently large matte plane.
candidates.rationale: Fills a real gap (all existing light attributes are magnitude statistics) with classic anchors; openly states that true vector illuminance is unmeasurable from a photo — proxy declared. AMBER accordingly.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Brightness variance, glare, vertical illuminance are all magnitude statistics; direction/diffuseness is unmeasured.

candidates.name: wall_luminance_dominance
candidates.construct: Flynn's lighting-mode impressions: peripheral (wall) light emphasis drives judged spaciousness and pleasantness; overhead-only light with dark walls reads smaller and less pleasant — WHERE light lands, not how much.
literature: Flynn, Spencer, Martyniuk & Hendrick 1973 (J. IES): wall lighting increased rated spaciousness; luminance placement reliably shifted impressions of the same room
Flynn 1977 (LD+A, hedged): consolidation of spaciousness-with-bright-walls modes
Loe, Mansfield & Rowlands 1994 (Lighting Research and Technology): the horizontal 40-deg band (wall-dominated) drives visual lightness and interest judgments
candidates.computation: Tier A + plane labels. wall_dominance = mean pseudo-luminance over wall-plane pixels / mean over all classified plane pixels; ceiling and floor ratios likewise; optional Loe horizontal mid-band variant. Output normalized [0,~2], >1 = wall-washed peripheral emphasis. ABSTAIN when wall planes <10% of classified pixels.
candidates.rationale: Classic, well-anchored construct; trivial deterministic computation over existing segmentation; orthogonal to vertical illuminance magnitude (dim wall-washed vs bright downlit can match at the eye).
candidates.collision_with_existing: Vertical illuminance proxy = magnitude at the eye; this = distribution across room surfaces.

candidates.name: visible_window_wall_ratio
candidates.construct: Window presence and relative size in the visual field — windowlessness is among the strongest negative findings in hospital-environment research (delirium, anxiety), independent of view content or illuminance.
literature: Keep, James & Inman 1980 (Anaesthesia): ~2x delirium/hallucination incidence in a windowless vs windowed ICU
Verderber 1986 (Environment and Behavior): window size/proximity/sill height systematically related to patient and staff wellbeing
Ulrich, Zimring et al. 2008 (HERD review): window access repeatedly associated with reduced stress
candidates.computation: Tier A + plane segmentation. Rectify each wall plane via homography; window candidates = quadrilateral subregions with median luminance above wall median by threshold, or containing sky/vegetation content (from V5/V3 detectors). WWR_visible = window area / total rectified wall-plane area, plus binary any_window flag. ABSTAIN when no wall plane segmented or glare blowout destroys the boundary.
candidates.rationale: Simple, outcome-anchored presence/size variable no existing attribute encodes (windows currently exist only as light sources or plan distances); complements V5 content scoring.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Glare/vertical illuminance treat windows as light sources; C10 is plan distance. Presence/relative size is new.

candidates.name: threshold_doorway_density
candidates.construct: Permeability and event-boundary rhythm: doorway/threshold density along main circulation — carries Gehl-grade frontage permeability (vitality) AND the Radvansky doorway effect (event-segmentation memory purge); the two links are deliberately reported separately, never averaged.
literature: Gehl 2010 (Cities for People): doors/units per 100 m as the operational active-frontage criterion
Ellard 2015 (Places of the Heart): the lively NYC frontage condition was specifically permeable (many openings)
Radvansky & Copeland 2006 (Memory & Cognition); Radvansky, Krawietz & Tamplin 2011 (QJEP): walking through doorways causes forgetting — the boundary itself drives the purge
Zacks & Swallow 2007 (Current Directions): event segmentation theory; thresholds as canonical boundaries
candidates.computation: Tier B primary: wall runs from the plan grid; doorway gaps = traversable interruptions ~0.7-1.5 m wide; medial-axis circulation spine; density = thresholds per 10 m of spine + spacing histogram. Tier A corroborator: door-shaped vertical rectangles (aspect ~1:2.0-2.4, floor-connected) via plane segmentation + Hough grouping; disagreement beyond tolerance flags and downweights. Inverted-U on the vitality reading; raw density and event-boundary count emitted separately.
candidates.rationale: Counts a thing (thresholds) genuinely invisible to C4 (a straight-corridor doorway is not a decision point); two literatures honestly kept apart. AMBER: plan inference + heuristic door detection.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C4 counts route-choice junctions only; no existing metric counts thresholds.

candidates.name: choice_point_option_differentiation
candidates.construct: Weisman's architectural differentiation at decision points: when co-visible route options look alike, walkers cannot encode which branch they took; discriminable options support route learning and retrace.
literature: Weisman 1981 (Environment and Behavior): architectural differentiation among four legibility factors predicting disorientation
Lynch 1960: look-alike corridors as a canonical cause of lostness
O'Neill 1991 (Environment and Behavior): perceptual differentiation variables affect wayfinding performance and cognitive-map accuracy
candidates.computation: Tier A + plane segmentation. Detect visible route openings (floor-plane continuation through wall gaps, door-height apertures); require >=2 openings else ABSTAIN (predicate fires only at junction views). Per opening: Lab means, color histograms, LBP texture of visible interior, aperture aspect, luminance. Score = mean pairwise dissimilarity (chi-square + normalized Euclidean) mapped to [0,1], plus n_options; low score at multi-option views = look-alike-corridor flag.
candidates.rationale: Orthogonal to C4 (quantity of choices vs discriminability of choices) with solid legibility-literature anchoring; disciplined abstention when not at a junction. AMBER: opening detection rides segmentation.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C4 measures decision LOAD; this measures whether the co-visible choices are perceptually discriminable.

candidates.name: signage_salience
candidates.construct: Visibility and conspicuity of signage/pictograms at decision-relevant placement — Weisman's third legibility factor and the one intervention experimentally shown to raise wayfinding accuracy, largest in low-legibility plans; the visible MITIGATION for high C4.
literature: O'Neill 1991 (Environment and Behavior): signage improved wayfinding accuracy, largest gains in least-legible plans
Weisman 1981: signage among the legibility cue classes predicting performance
Arthur & Passini 1992 (Wayfinding: People, Signs, and Architecture): conspicuity = contrast x size at distance x placement in the line of travel
candidates.computation: Tier A, classical only. Text-like regions via MSER + stroke-width-transform consistency (no learned model); pictogram/plaque candidates via compact high-contrast quadrilaterals on wall/ceiling planes. Conspicuity = Weber contrast x angular-size proxy (depth-corrected if available) x placement weight (movement axis / eye-height band from VP horizon). Outputs: count, max and summed conspicuity 0-1; zero candidates scores 0 with an explicit single-frame coverage caveat. Reported jointly with C4: high decision load + zero visible signage is the actionable finding.
candidates.rationale: Two-seat convergence; experimentally anchored intervention variable absent from the stack; AMBER honestly kept — SWT cannot distinguish directional signage from posters, and one frame samples the signage system.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Nothing existing touches signage or text; C4 gets its designable mitigation variable.

candidates.name: eye_level_information_richness
candidates.construct: Information density of vertical surfaces in the pedestrian fixation band (~1.0-1.8 m): low richness where the walking gaze rests predicts boredom, reduced dwelling, faster transit (Ellard/Gehl), with an autonomic under-stimulation cost.
literature: Ellard 2015 (Places of the Heart): blank vs rich frontage — lower arousal/affect (self-report + skin conductance) and faster moving-on at the blank facade
Gehl, Kaefer & Reigstad 2006 (Urban Design International): ground-floor articulation drives pedestrian pace and pausing
Cavalcante et al. 2014 (PLoS ONE): streetscape complexity quantifiable from local contrast and spatial-frequency statistics that track judgments
Merrifield & Danckert 2014 (Exp. Brain Research): induced boredom elevates cortisol — the wellbeing side
candidates.computation: Tier A. Eye-level band from the VP horizon (fallback central 0.40-0.60 height band with uncertainty flag); mask to wall/facade planes; ~32px column tiles scoring Canny edge density, gradient-orientation entropy, local RMS contrast; band-mean richness corpus-normalized 0-1. ABSTAIN with no visible vertical surfaces.
candidates.rationale: Spatially targeted and valenced oppositely at the low end relative to the global clutter proxy (under-stimulation risk); field-physiology anchoring is small-N but direct. AMBER: rides VP/segmentation.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Global clutter proxy treats information as cost everywhere; this is band-restricted and detects the dead-wall condition a calm ceiling/floor would mask.

candidates.name: edge_following_affordance
candidates.construct: Thigmotaxis support: whether the plan permits movement and pausing along wall edges rather than forcing exposed center crossings — edge-following is a robust anxiety-regulating locomotion strategy, and edges are where people linger (Gehl edge effect).
literature: Kallai et al. 2005, 2007 (Behavioural Brain Research): human wall-hugging in arena navigation is a robust early strategy; persistence correlates with trait anxiety
Gehl 2011 (Life Between Buildings): people overwhelmingly stand/linger at edges of open spaces; centers fill last
Appleton 1975: refuge-with-prospect frame (theory anchor; seat-level twin already implemented as C11)
candidates.computation: Tier B. Edge cells = traversable cells 8-adjacent to walls/fixed obstacles. Outputs: edge coverage (fraction of open-space perimeter that is traversable), edge continuity (longest connected edge component / perimeter), center-forcing index (fraction of sampled entry-entry/entry-seat shortest paths forced off the edge band for >~2 m). Composite = mean(coverage, continuity, 1−center-forcing), 0-1. ABSTAIN on low plan confidence or corridor-only scenes.
candidates.rationale: Evaluates LOCOMOTION protection, which seated C11 and C16 cannot see (a room can score well on seated refuge yet force exposed crossings); solid behavioral anchoring. AMBER: fully downstream of plan inference.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C11 is stationary seat-level; C16 is ownership. Moving-along-edges affordance is new.

candidates.name: scaling_coherence_octaves
candidates.construct: Whether self-similar structure is actually continuous across scales (Salingaros/Alexander scale hierarchy) — doubling as a validity guard: a fitted fractal D is spurious when octave slopes are inconsistent.
literature: Salingaros & West 1999 (Environment and Planning B): inverse-power distribution of structural scales in coherent architecture; scale-hierarchy gaps predicted incoherent (theory-grade)
Taylor, Micolich & Jonas 1999 (Nature): genuine fractal scaling requires consistent box-count slope across scales — the methodological basis
Alexander 2001 (The Nature of Order): levels of scale (theoretical anchor only)
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic arithmetic on the EXISTING box-count table; sandbox-verified by the proposing seat (Sierpinski scores 1.0; single-scale grid scores 0.77 despite a fittable 'D'). Per-octave local slopes d logN/d log eps; coherence = 1 − std/|mean| of octave slopes, clipped [0,1]; also global-fit R^2 and worst-deviating octave (which scale band is missing).
candidates.rationale: Zero new image processing, directly upgrades the honesty of the existing fractal D and of V9; construct link is theory-grade (hence AMBER despite trivially clean computation — ceiling preserved as proposed).
candidates.collision_with_existing: Existing fractal D silently assumes log-log linearity; this tests it and flags spurious values.

candidates.name: curved_sightline_promise
candidates.construct: Photographic mystery cues: deflected vistas (sightline bending out of view), partial occlusion at the deepest visible region, and lit apertures into partially visible adjacent space — the Tier-A twin of V4 for when plan inference fails.
literature: Kaplan & Kaplan 1989: curved paths and screened views were the prototypical mystery stimuli
Herzog & Bryce 2007 (Environment and Behavior, hedged): curved vs straight pathway views reliably raised preference
Gimblett, Itami & Fitzgibbon 1985 (hedged): screening and sightline curvature predicted landscape preference
candidates.computation: Tier A over existing plane segmentation + frozen depth. (1) Floor-wall boundary polylines toward the VP: mean absolute curvature and total heading change (bending corridor scores high). (2) Occlusion test at the deepest floor region: vertical occluding edge with floor continuing behind (depth discontinuity) vs frontal wall plateau. (3) Lit-aperture cue: portrait-aspect wall quadrilaterals with large depth discontinuity and interior luminance above threshold. Weighted sum of three 0-1 cues; ABSTAIN when no floor path or aperture confidently detected.
candidates.rationale: Complementary fallback to V4 with its own cue-level literature; measures the SHAPE of the sightline's termination, which scalar prospect cannot distinguish. AMBER: rides depth model + segmentation.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Prospect scores maximum depth; two photos of equal depth can differ maximally on bend/occlusion/aperture cues.

candidates.name: scene_coherence_grouping
candidates.construct: Coherence (Kaplan preference matrix; Coburn/Vartanian coherence factor): how readily the scene organizes into a few repeated, contiguous groups — organization/redundancy, distinct from feature quantity — with a geometric orientation-concentration sub-score.
literature: Kaplan & Kaplan 1989: coherence ('hangs together') as a preference-matrix predictor and precondition for extent
Kotabe, Kardan & Berman 2016 (JEP: General, hedged title): perceived disorder driven by low-level spatial features independent of semantics — supports low-level operationalization
Stamps 2004 (hedged): coherence reliably positive for preference, distinct from complexity
Coburn et al. 2020: coherence as one of three principal dimensions of architectural experience
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic, fixed-seed. SLIC superpixels -> per-superpixel Lab color, edge-orientation histogram, texture energy -> agglomerative clustering at fixed threshold. coherence = w1(1−normalized cluster count) + w2(mean spatial contiguity of clusters) + w3(repetition score: clusters with >=3 separated instances). Sub-score (from the merged Chatterjee variant): fraction of edge length aligned to dominant vanishing directions + orientation-histogram negentropy, for Manhattan-structured scenes. Output 0-1.
candidates.rationale: Two seats converged on the coherence construct with complementary operationalizations, folded into one candidate (grouping primary — generalizes to non-rectilinear interiors; orientation concentration as sub-score). AMBER: grouping pipeline is a proxy for a rating-defined construct.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Clutter proxy = congestion (amount), palette entropy = color variety; coherence = ORGANIZATION. Dense-but-patterned vs sparse-but-mismatched rooms dissociate them.

candidates.name: isovist_jaggedness
candidates.construct: Perceived spatial complexity of the visible VOLUME (convolutedness of the spatial envelope), as opposed to surface/texture clutter — perimeter²/area of the vantage isovist.
literature: Franz & Wiener 2008 (Environment and Planning B): isovist jaggedness correlated with rated spatial complexity and negatively with clarity/pleasingness in controlled VR interiors
Wiener & Franz 2005 (Spatial Cognition IV): isovist scalars predicted experiential ratings and navigation behavior
Psarra & Grajewski 2001 (Space Syntax Symposium): boundary shape-complexity along paths
candidates.computation: Tier B, from the V4 ray-cast isovist polygon: J = P²/(4πA), report log(J); secondary output = count of reflex vertices after Douglas-Peucker simplification (epsilon = 1 grid cell). Deterministic; abstains with the plan grid.
candidates.rationale: Direct VR-experimental construct link; free once V4's isovist exists; dissociates alcoved-minimalist from poster-covered-box conditions the 2D clutter proxy conflates.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Clutter proxy = image texture density; C2 = graph degree. Envelope shape complexity is new.

candidates.name: maintenance_dilapidation_index
candidates.construct: Visible building care: stains, discolored/missing ceiling tiles, cracked/peeling finishes. In POE, maintenance is a penalty-dominant 'basic factor' — poor states drive dissatisfaction strongly; perceived care underlies the Leaman/Bordass forgiveness effect.
literature: Kim & de Dear 2013 (Building and Environment): cleanliness/maintenance a nonlinear dissatisfier across a large POE database
Leaman & Bordass 1999 (Building Research & Information): perceived management care predicts forgiveness and reported productivity
Vilnai-Yavetz, Rafaeli & Schneider Yaacov 2005 (Environment and Behavior): upkeep shapes employee affect
Wilson & Kelling 1982: disorder-signaling mechanism only (urban, adjacent)
candidates.computation: Tier A + plane segmentation. Per wall/ceiling plane: fit low-order illumination model to luminance; residual map -> low-frequency blobs beyond k·sigma = stain candidates; high-frequency edge clusters on nominally smooth planes = crack/peel candidates; suspended-ceiling grid (regular Hough spacing) enables per-tile deviation scoring. Output anomaly-area fraction, penalty-shaped nonlinearly per Kim & de Dear. ABSTAIN when wall+ceiling planes <15% of image.
candidates.rationale: Strong POE anchoring with the right (penalty-dominant) response shape; first surface-condition attribute in the stack. AMBER: shadows and intentional texture (exposed brick, art) are acknowledged confounds.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Nothing existing inspects surface condition; the acoustic material table assumes intact materials.

candidates.name: visible_workstation_density
candidates.construct: Photographic occupancy-density cue: workstations per visible floor area and nearest-neighbor desk spacing — density/close seating among the most robust negative predictors of workspace satisfaction and privacy.
literature: Oldham & Rotchford 1983 (ASQ): higher density and lower interpersonal distance -> lower satisfaction, more withdrawal
Oldham & Fried 1987 (J. Applied Psychology): dark, unenclosed, close-seated, many-occupant workspaces produced the most negative reactions
Brennan, Chugh & Kline 2002 (Environment and Behavior): satisfaction dropped after a denser open-plan move, no 6-month recovery
Kim & de Dear 2013: privacy/noise (density-mediated) dominant open-plan dissatisfiers
candidates.computation: Tier A+B. Count chair/desk/monitor instances (frozen instance segmentation, already in stack); back-project chair centroids onto the recovered floor plane via heuristic depth/scale; workstations per ~100 m² (proxy-scaled) + median nearest-neighbor spacing. Classical fallback: edge-map autocorrelation along vanishing directions (lower confidence, flagged). ABSTAIN without floor plane or <2 candidates.
candidates.rationale: Strong POE literature; its second value is as an independent cross-check on C12 — photo-vs-plan disagreement is itself an inference-quality flag for the Tier-B pipeline. AMBER twice: pretrained segmentation + heuristic metric scale.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C12 crowding is computed on the INFERRED plan; this is independent photographic furniture evidence — agreement is diagnostic, declared.

candidates.name: peripheral_crowding_load
candidates.construct: At-a-glance legibility under crowding: how much content falls in eccentricity-scaled pooling regions whose heterogeneity exceeds what summary-statistic peripheral vision preserves — the bottleneck for noticing signage/landmarks you are not fixating.
literature: Bouma 1970 (Nature): critical spacing ~0.5x eccentricity — the pooling geometry used
Balas, Nakano & Rosenholtz 2009 (Journal of Vision): summary-statistic ('mongrel') account of crowding
Rosenholtz et al. 2012 (Journal of Vision): same representation explains search difficulty
Freeman & Simoncelli 2011 (Nature Neuroscience): physiologically grounded eccentricity-scaled pooling
Whitney & Levi 2011 (TiCS): crowding as the fundamental peripheral limit
candidates.computation: Tier A + declared viewing geometry (FOV constant; fixation default = image center or existing VP). Radial-annular pooling regions with radius ~0.4-0.5x eccentricity; per region: circular variance of gradient-orientation histogram + Lab covariance volume. Load = area-weighted heterogeneity summed beyond ~2 deg, corpus-normalized 0-1, plus inspectable region map. ABSTAIN when no defensible fixation exists.
candidates.rationale: The only proposal modeling eccentricity-dependent vision — a real mechanism all fixation-agnostic attributes miss; AMBER honestly for the uncertified FOV/fixation assumptions and simplified summary statistics.
candidates.collision_with_existing: All existing Tier-A attributes weight image locations equally; none models peripheral pooling.

candidates.name: isovist_radial_asymmetry
candidates.construct: Shape of spaciousness: whether visible openness is balanced around the observer or concentrated directionally (elongation/eccentricity), and whether the depth distribution is short- or long-tailed (skewness) — rotunda vs shaft at equal area.
literature: Benedikt 1979: variance and skewness of radial lengths as core isovist descriptors because area under-describes felt character
Stamps ~2009-2011 (Environment and Behavior, hedged): perceived spaciousness responds to elongation/aspect, not floor area alone
Franz & Wiener 2008: radial statistics tracked spaciousness ratings in VR interiors
candidates.computation: Tier B, from V4 radials: skewness = m3/m2^1.5 (clipped [−3,3]); elongation = r_max/median(r) or PCA-ellipse eccentricity of the isovist polygon. Deterministic; abstains with the plan grid.
candidates.rationale: Cheap add-on to the V4 isovist machinery capturing distributional SHAPE of openness that C24 (magnitude contrast) and prospect (axial depth) miss.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C24 uses openness-contrast magnitude; prospect is axial. Balanced-vs-directional and tail shape are new.

candidates.name: isovist_drift
candidates.construct: Directional pull of space at the vantage: vector from observer to isovist centroid — the local egocentric movement cue (does the volume's center of gravity sit where you stand or pull you elsewhere), including alignment with the photographed view axis.
literature: Conroy Dalton 2001 (UCL PhD) and Dalton & Dalton 2001 (Space Syntax Symposium, OmniVista): drift defined; VE participants re-orient where drift changes and move toward the visual center of gravity
Wiener, Franz et al. 2007 (Perception): isovist properties at decision points capture movement-relevant spatial properties
candidates.computation: Tier B. d = centroid(isovist) − camera cell. Outputs: |d|/mean(r) (0 balanced to >1 strong pull) and alignment = cos(angle between d and the recovered view axis) (+1 = space pulls where the photo looks; near −1 = main volume behind/beside = latent disorientation cue). Deterministic.
candidates.rationale: Genuine local egocentric cue absent from global C4; evidence base is thinner (thesis + symposium) than sibling isovist scalars, hence mid rank.
candidates.collision_with_existing: C4 is a global plan graph measure; local pull at the photographed vantage is new.

candidates.name: blank_frontage_run_length
candidates.construct: Boredom dose from sustained featureless wall: the longest contiguous low-information run at eye level — the worst-case statistic Ellard's walkers responded to and Gehl's per-100m codes regulate, which any mean dilutes.
literature: Ellard 2015: the blank condition was a long unbroken wall; the deficit tied to sustained absence of visual events while moving along it
Gehl 2010: frontage graded by units/doors per 100 m; long unbroken frontage = inactive
Gehl, Kaefer & Reigstad 2006: faster walking, less stopping along unarticulated ground floors
candidates.computation: Tier A, on V28's band tiles. Tile 'blank' if edge density AND orientation entropy below corpus 25th percentiles; close single-tile gaps; longest contiguous blank run as fraction of visible band width (0-1, higher worse). With Tier-B depth available, convert to approximate metres against Gehl thresholds; else metric length flagged UNKNOWN. ABSTAIN with V28.
candidates.rationale: Cheap child of V28 adding the max-run statistic means cannot express; ranked below its parent since it shares all its assumptions.
candidates.collision_with_existing: All existing and proposed mean-based measures dilute the dead-wall run; none carries a worst-case run length.

candidates.name: vista_termination_salience
candidates.construct: Lynch's directional differentiation: whether the visual axis of a path terminates in a distinct, articulated element (anchoring and memorable) versus a blank wall or ambiguous continuation.
literature: Lynch 1960: path imageability depends on clear termination; termini anchor paths
Conroy Dalton 2003 (Environment and Behavior): walkers track the visual axis ahead (least-angle choice), so its terminus carries orientation information
Cullen 1961 (Townscape): vista closure as a device of legible sequence (design theory, proxy-grade)
candidates.computation: Tier A over VP + depth. Terminal patch = region around the VP within the top depth decile; salience vs surrounding annulus = luminance contrast + color chi-square + edge-density ratio; classify LANDMARK-TERMINATED / BLANK / OPEN-CONTINUATION (depth saturates at frame edge or VP falls in an aperture). Output class + 0-1 salience. ABSTAIN without a stable VP.
candidates.rationale: Positional/directional property the existing landmark salience cannot express; anchors mix experimental (Dalton) and design-theory (Cullen) evidence. AMBER: VP/depth chain.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Landmark salience scores elements anywhere in frame; termination of the movement axis specifically is new.

candidates.name: view_depth_layering
candidates.construct: Depth stratification of the visible scene: count of distinct foreground/midground/background depth layers (multimodality of the depth distribution) — Ulrich's 'preferenda' include depth/spatial definition; a deep but layerless view differs from a layered one at equal prospect.
literature: Ulrich 1983 (in Altman & Wohlwill): depth/spatial definition among gross structural properties driving immediate affective response
Kaplan & Kaplan 1989: inferred depth and layered occlusion as preference predictors
Ko, Schiavon et al. ~2020 (Building and Environment): window view improved working memory and emotion — view structure, not just light
candidates.computation: Tier A on the frozen depth map (optionally restricted to a detected glazing region). KDE of log-depth; count prominence-thresholded modes (clip 1-4) + depth-range ratio p95/p5. ABSTAIN on low depth-model confidence or sub-minimum glazing area.
candidates.rationale: Clean geometric complement to V5 (semantic layers) and to scalar prospect; preference-grade construct link and full dependence on the frozen depth model keep it mid-ranked.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Prospect is scalar depth magnitude; multimodality of the distribution is new (blank wall far away = high prospect, 1 layer).

candidates.name: egress_aperture_salience
candidates.construct: Visible escape/egress affordance: navigable floor-connected openings — the behavioral pole of Vartanian's enclosure findings (avoidance tracks perceived possibility of leaving) and Fisher-Nasar's 'escape' factor in fear reduction.
literature: Vartanian et al. 2013 (PNAS): enclosed interiors elicited more exit/avoidance decisions
Fisher & Nasar 1992 (Environment and Behavior): escape independently lowers fear in the prospect-refuge-escape triad
Appleton 1975: refuge without escape reads as trap (proxy-grade support)
candidates.computation: Tier A + plane segmentation. Aperture candidates at wall boundaries: door-shaped floor-connected openings (taller than wide, low interior luminance = navigable) and bright floor-connected far openings; EXCLUDE high windows (view without egress). Salience = normalized sum of subtended angular sizes, weighted by lateral position. 0-1. ABSTAIN when wall planes unrecoverable.
candidates.rationale: Distinct behavioral predictor (egress, not view depth) with experimental anchoring; overlaps V19's locomotive channel conceptually but is the photographic vantage-level variant — kept for Tier-A coverage. AMBER: aperture detection.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Prospect measures how far the eye travels; navigable exits are what carried the avoidance and fear effects.

candidates.name: material_perceived_warmth
candidates.construct: Perceived warmth of surface materials as a joint function of color, roughness, and gloss — Wastiels showed texture contributes to material warmth independently of hue; a semantic material property beyond the chromatic warm/cool ratio.
literature: Wastiels, Schifferstein, Heylighen & Martens 2012 (Materials & Design): warmth of building materials predicted jointly by color AND roughness/gloss
Fujisaki et al. ~2015 (Vision Research, hedged): wood material perception strongly driven by visual surface statistics
Nyrud & Bringslimark 2010: warmth among the most consistent semantic attributions to visible wood
candidates.computation: Tier A per segmented region: (1) chromatic warmth (signed hue distance from warm axis, lightness-weighted); (2) roughness proxy (mid-band RMS luminance energy); (3) gloss proxy (specular blob density, luminance kurtosis). Warmth = w1·chroma + w2·rough − w3·gloss, area-weighted, 0-1; weight SIGNS from Wastiels' regression, magnitudes declared defaults (stated as such).
candidates.rationale: Directly cited multi-dimensional regression basis; honest about declared (not fitted) weight magnitudes; image roughness is a proxy for physical roughness.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Warm/cool ratio ignores the two non-chromatic warmth dimensions Wastiels established — declared extension.

candidates.name: spectral_expansion
candidates.construct: Spatial-envelope expansion: strength of linear perspective / depth gradient — how strongly the scene funnels attention and implied movement in one direction (corridor vs frontal hall at equal view depth); a Tier-A photo-level feed for the wayfinding story.
literature: Oliva & Torralba 2001 (IJCV): expansion as a spatial-envelope dimension of man-made scenes, recoverable from oriented spectral energy layout
Ross & Oliva 2010 (Journal of Vision): human perspective/depth-gradient estimates predicted from global features
Gibson 1979: optic structure specifies locomotion affordance — expansion->movement-pull link is theoretical, stated as proxy
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic. Per-block dominant Gabor orientations over a 4x4/8x8 grid; least-squares fit of a radial convergence field; expansion = convergence goodness-of-fit x vertical texture-gradient magnitude; cross-checked against the existing VP detector (consistency/strength of convergence). 0-1.
candidates.rationale: Deterministic and complementary to prospect (funneling vs distance), but the construct link to wayfinding pull is theoretically motivated rather than behaviorally measured — honestly proxy-grade.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Prospect = how far; expansion = how strongly geometry directs. Equal-depth corridor vs hall dissociate them.

candidates.name: proto_object_set_size
candidates.construct: Effective set size at the object level: count of segmentable proto-object chunks — the object-count face of clutter driving classic set-size search effects and the felt 'too many things'.
literature: Yu, Samaras & Zelinsky 2014 (Journal of Vision): proto-object count from superpixel merging predicts clutter ratings, outperforming pixel measures on their dataset
Wolfe 1998 (Psychological Science): search RT scales with set size
Rosenholtz, Li & Nakano 2007: motivates object-level complement where true object counts are ill-defined
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic. SLIC (~600 segments, compactness 10) -> agglomerative merge of adjacent superpixels with deltaE < T (T fixed, sensitivity at ±20% emitted) -> count of components above minimum area; also per-steradian density under declared FOV. Cross-check with V7 — divergence is itself diagnostic (many discrete objects vs few chaotic surfaces).
candidates.rationale: The only count over discrete chunks (what set-size predictions and C4 reasoning consume); AMBER for the heuristic merge threshold and single-lab validation.
candidates.collision_with_existing: All clutter-adjacent attributes are pixel statistics; a discrete-chunk count is new.

candidates.name: nested_symmetry_index
candidates.construct: Symmetry nesting (Alexander's local symmetries / Salingaros sub-symmetries): symmetry at multiple scales with hierarchically aligned axes, as distinct from one global mirror axis.
literature: Alexander & Carey 1968 (Perception & Psychophysics): subsymmetry count predicts perceived pattern goodness — the one direct psychophysical anchor for nesting
Jacobsen & Hoefel 2003: symmetry dominates beauty judgments of novel patterns (single-scale)
Makin, Pecchinenda & Bertamini 2012 (+ SPN work): symmetry elicits implicit positive affect and reliable neural response
Alexander 2001: theoretical anchor for the architectural claim
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic. 4-level pyramid of gradient magnitude; sliding-window NCC with horizontal mirror per level -> peak maps; nesting = child peaks whose axes fall inside a parent peak's symmetric span within tolerance; index = participating levels / available levels, strength-weighted, plus nested-chain count. ABSTAIN when the VP step marks the view strongly oblique (foreshortening attenuates true symmetry — declared bias).
candidates.rationale: Genuine multi-scale extension of the existing single-scale horizontal symmetry with one direct psychophysical anchor; interior extrapolation and oblique-view attenuation keep it AMBER and mid-rank.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Existing horizontal symmetry is global, single-scale; axis-aligned nesting is the actual Alexander/Salingaros claim.

candidates.name: phog_self_similarity
candidates.construct: Pyramid self-similarity of oriented gradient content: whether the KIND of structure (orientation distribution) repeats across scales — the Redies-lab statistic artworks and natural scenes share, distinct from box-count occupancy scaling.
literature: Amirshahi, Koch, Denzler & Redies 2012: artworks show high PHOG self-similarity comparable to natural scenes
Braun, Amirshahi, Denzler & Redies 2013 (Frontiers in Psychology): architecture photographs characterized — norms for built-space images exist
Redies 2007: aesthetic images converge on natural-scene statistics (correlational, proxy-grade)
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic. HOG orientation histograms (16 bins, magnitude-weighted) on a spatial pyramid (whole image + 4/16/64 cells); self-similarity = mean histogram-intersection between each child cell and its parent; output mean + level profile, 0-1.
candidates.rationale: Clean deterministic statistic dissociable from fractal D (striped wall vs richly self-similar scene can share D); preference link correlational and interiors characterized but not preference-tested — AMBER, mid rank.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Fractal D = binarized-edge occupancy scaling; orientation-content self-similarity is a different scale-invariance.

candidates.name: hominess_composite
candidates.construct: Hominess — perceived homelikeness/comfort/warmth-of-dwelling, the third Coburn/Vartanian factor of architectural experience, loading on warm low-key light and soft materials, dissociable from mere warm color.
literature: Coburn et al. 2020: hominess a robust separable factor of interior experience, dissociable from coherence and fascination
Vartanian et al. 2013 and subsequent: comfort/approach tracks soft, enclosing, warm qualities
Meyers-Levy & Zhu 2007 (adjacent): felt quality, not measured brightness, shapes response
candidates.computation: Tier A + material/plane segmentation. Z-scored proxies fused 0-1: (a) warm low-key luminance (warm-hued AND mid-to-low luminance pixels, excluding glare-white); (b) material softness (inverse specular-blob density + mid-frequency fabric/grain texture energy); (c) soft-material fraction from the existing material table. ABSTAIN without material segmentation.
candidates.rationale: Real factor-analytic construct, but a multi-proxy composite with invented fusion weights riding material segmentation — the weakest computational grounding among the Coburn-factor proposals.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Warm/cool ratio is purely chromatic; the material-softness and low-key-luminance loadings are new.

candidates.name: desk_personalization_index
candidates.construct: Visible territorial personalization of workstations (personal objects on desks): correlates with satisfaction, wellbeing, and organizational identification; suppression (clean-desk regimes) with dissatisfaction — the visible-appropriation complement to plan-level C16.
literature: Wells 2000 (J. Environmental Psychology): personalization associated with wellbeing/satisfaction
Knight & Haslam 2010 (JEP: Applied): enriched and self-decorated offices beat lean on comfort, wellbeing, productivity
Brunia & Hartjes-Gosselink 2009: personalization persists even in hot-desk offices — basic territorial need
candidates.computation: Tier A + planes + depth. Horizontal planes in the desk-height band (~0.6-1.1 m via geometry); connected components deviating from the fitted surface color, kept in a 0.2-8% area band. Outputs: small-object density per surface area + hue entropy of blobs (personal items chromatically diverse), both 0-1. ABSTAIN with no desk-height plane. Declared: measures object richness on work surfaces — a proxy that cannot separate personal items from equipment or mess.
candidates.rationale: Decent experimental base (Knight & Haslam), honest proxy declaration; detector fragility (equipment/mess confound) keeps it mid-rank.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Existing clutter proxy treats objects as global cost; C16 knows nothing of visible appropriation — surface-localized, positively valenced complement.

candidates.name: spectral_openness
candidates.construct: Spatial-envelope openness from global spectral statistics — a segmentation-free estimator of felt spaciousness whose principal value is TRIANGULATION: agreement with the plane-seg enclosure index raises joint confidence, disagreement raises an honest uncertainty flag.
literature: Oliva & Torralba 2001 (IJCV): openness recoverable from windowed Fourier/Gabor energy templates
Greene & Oliva 2009: openness perceived from <100 ms glances
Ross & Oliva 2010 (Journal of Vision): layout-property ratings predicted from global features
Stamps 2000s (hedged): enclosure/spaciousness driven by boundary proportion/permeability — interior transfer support
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic. Windowed FFT + 4x4 Gabor grid (GIST-like); fixed logistic over two hand-set Oliva-Torralba discriminants (low-SF horizontal-orientation energy fraction; vertical gradient of texture energy). 0-1, plus a DISAGREEMENT flag when rank order conflicts strongly with the enclosure index.
candidates.rationale: Its output is mostly epistemic (an independent cross-check on an AMBER pipeline) rather than new construct signal; validated mainly on outdoor scene sets — interior transfer proxy-grade, honestly flagged.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Same construct family as the enclosure index by design; declared value is statistically independent triangulation.

candidates.name: spectral_mean_depth
candidates.construct: Ordinal scene size class (near / room-scale / hall-scale) from global spectral signature — a training-free, statistically independent cross-check whose main product is an honesty flag on the frozen-depth-model prospect pipeline.
literature: Torralba & Oliva 2002 (IEEE TPAMI): mean depth predicted from global spectral signatures
Ross & Oliva 2010: human mean-depth judgments predicted from the same features
Greene & Oliva 2009: mean depth extracted at a glance
candidates.computation: Tier A, deterministic. Radial FFT energy distribution + block-wise dominant Gabor scale; map to ordinal bins (<2 m / 2-8 m / >8 m) via hand-set thresholds declared as calibration constants on a small labeled interior set (declared calibration, NOT reproduction of the paper's learned regression). If spectral bin and depth-net mean depth disagree by >1 bin, attach an uncertainty flag to prospect rather than reporting either confidently.
candidates.rationale: Like V50, chiefly an honesty instrument (single-point-of-failure guard on the depth net); ordinal only without the learned regression.
candidates.collision_with_existing: Prospect rests on the frozen depth model; this is its independent disagreement detector, declared as such.

candidates.name: size_hierarchy_exponent
candidates.construct: Salingaros-West universal size distribution: whether discrete visual elements follow an inverse-power size distribution (graded many-small/few-large hierarchy) versus mono-scale repetition or scale gaps — the ornament-hierarchy claim proper.
literature: Salingaros & West 1999 (Environment and Planning B): inverse-power component-size distribution (exponent ~2) in coherent built form — theoretical with illustrative measurements, stated as such
