The construct is real (Coburn et al. 2020's hominess factor exists), but the operationalization is invented at both levels: (1) no cited study provides a mapping from image features to hominess — unlike V43, where Wastiels supplies a regression fixing at least the weight signs across the exact predictor dimensions, here the choice of the three proxies AND their fusion weights are both fabricated, and the proposal admits it ('invented fusion weights', 'weakest computational grounding'). A number produced this way measures the author's intuition about hominess, not hominess. (2) Two of the three anchors are misused (see citation concern). (3) Computationally it stacks three noisy proxies (warm low-key luminance is exposure-confounded; 'fabric/grain texture energy' inherits every roughness-proxy failure from V43; soft-material fraction rides material segmentation) and then hides their individual error inside a weighted sum — the composite structure prevents the sensitivity honesty that saves V45. Fabrication is the cardinal sin; a composite whose loadings have no evidential source is a fabricated mapping with citations decorating it.
V48.citation_concern: Vartanian et al. 2013 is the CONTOUR study (curvilinear vs rectilinear -> beauty/approach); it does not show comfort/approach tracking 'soft, enclosing, warm' qualities — and the related Vartanian et al. 2015 enclosure work found enclosed spaces increased EXIT/avoidance decisions, the opposite valence of the enclosure loading implied here. Meyers-Levy & Zhu 2007 is ceiling-height priming of processing style (abstraction vs item-specific), sold here as a generic 'felt quality shapes response' gloss — tangential at best. Coburn et al. 2020 is real and correctly described.
V48.fixed_computation: Do not emit a hominess score. Emit the three sub-proxies separately as raw, individually-caveated indicators (warm low-key luminance fraction; specular-inverse softness proxy; soft-material fraction from the existing material table), and have the hominess attribute itself ABSTAIN with an explicit note: 'construct established (Coburn et al. 2020) but no literature-derived feature loadings exist; fusion withheld.' If David later obtains rated-interior data, weights can be fitted and the attribute promoted — that is the only evidence path to a fused score.

V55.reasons: Computability mostly holds: the score is a scale-invariant ratio, so monocular scale ambiguity is harmless, and VP-homography floor rectification is standard. Two failure modes must be handled explicitly: (a) specular reflection streaks — ceiling luminaires reflected in glossy corridor floors produce long, high-contrast, axis-aligned lines that Hough will read as guidance cues (glare masquerading as wayfinding information); (b) the 'movement axis' is silently assumed to be the camera axis, which is a photograph-framing assumption (photographers shoot down corridors) not a room property — in oblique or room-corner shots the metric measures composition, not circulation. Evidence side is honestly hedged and real: Lynch 1960 continuity is genuine imageability theory, Emo's eye-tracking work (c. 2014) genuinely reports wayfinding gaze concentrating on the floor plane and longest sightlines, and Arthur & Passini 1992 is correctly labeled design doctrine. The proposal claims AMBER (rides plane segmentation + VP), which matches reality, and the rank hedge (thin direct experimental base) is honest. Defensible as a hedged proxy with the fixes below.
V55.fixed_computation: Before Hough: mask specular/bright pixels (reuse the glare bright-patch detector) so reflected light streaks cannot count as floor cues. Gate on VP confidence and abstain when the dominant VP is far off-center (camera axis not plausibly the movement axis) — state the camera-axis≈movement-axis assumption in the attribute's uncertainty flag. Also abstain on heavily glossy floors where masked area exceeds a threshold of the rectified floor.
V55.citation_concern: Emo '~2014' should be pinned to the exact paper (Emo 2014, eye-tracking in wayfinding decision points; gaze toward floor plane and longest line of sight) — the finding is real but the tilde citation must be resolved before shipping.

V56.reasons: Fails computability at the construct level: none of the three sub-scores discriminates temporal depth from ordinary photographic and material confounds. Low-frequency chroma variance fires on lighting gradients and colored light spill; dark low-saturation blobs are exactly what cast shadows and soiling look like; and matte-ness from a single photo is not recoverable — perceived gloss depends on light-source geometry relative to the camera, so a matte wall lit raking reads glossy and a lacquered floor with no highlight in frame reads matte. The index would score a shadowed corner of new drywall as 'patina' and a pristine varnished antique as 'new.' Distinguishing genuine weathering from factory-distressed finish or dirt is semantics classical CV cannot deliver. It also depends on 'regions V12 classifies as natural' — V12 is a sibling candidate, not a shipped attribute; if V12 dies or underperforms, this abstains everywhere. Finally, the payload is empty for the WB code: by design it abstains on valence, so the well-being layer receives a descriptive tag it can do nothing with; 'abstention-pattern study value' is a research note, not a reason to ship a metric. The hue-abstention precedent doesn't rescue it — hue is trivially and reliably measurable with only the affect mapping abstained; here the measurement itself is unreliable. Literature is real but correctly self-labeled theory-grade (Kellert's 'patina of time' is an attribute list, not an empirical effect), so there is no evidence anchor strong enough to justify the computability risk.
V56.fixed_computation: Not fixable within Tier A. An honest version needs a declared extra input (material provenance/finish metadata or a surveyed material map with age/finish fields), at which point it is a lookup, not a CV attribute. If the team wants the Kellert pattern represented, record it as a declared-input Tier attribute, not an image computation.
V56.citation_concern: None invented: Kellert 2008 / Kellert & Calabrese 2015 ('age, change, and the patina of time' as a biophilic attribute), Nyrud & Bringslimark 2010 (review of psychological responses to interior wood), and Broman ~2001 (heterogeneous appreciation of knotty wood surfaces) are all real and honestly graded — but note all are preference/theory literature; none links measured patina to any well-being outcome.

V49.reasons: Evidence is the strongest in the batch: Wells 2000 (JEP) is a real personalization–wellbeing study, Knight & Haslam 2010 (JEP: Applied) is a genuine experiment where enriched/self-decorated offices beat lean ones, and Brunia & Hartjes-Gosselink 2009 (J. Corporate Real Estate) really documents persistence of personalization in non-territorial offices. The computation is honest about being 'object richness on work surfaces', which is the right declaration because classical CV genuinely cannot separate a framed family photo from a stapler — the proposal never pretends otherwise. Remaining weaknesses to keep on record: (a) the desk-height band (0.6–1.1 m) requires metric scale from monocular depth + camera-height assumptions, a known AMBER fragility; (b) one photo samples one or two desks, while the construct (a personalization regime, e.g. clean-desk policy) is office-level — a staged/showroom photo scores 0 for reasons unrelated to policy; (c) there is a latent tension with the existing clutter proxy: the same blobs score as cost there and as positive appropriation here, so the two must be reported jointly, not summed. None of these are fatal given the declared proxy status. Rides planes+depth, so the claimed AMBER ceiling is exactly right.

V50.reasons: All four anchors are real and correctly characterized (Oliva & Torralba 2001 IJCV spatial-envelope openness; Greene & Oliva 2009 glance-speed global properties; Ross & Oliva 2010 J. Vision layout-property estimation; Stamps' enclosure meta-analytic work, appropriately hedged). The role claimed — triangulation/disagreement flag against the plane-seg enclosure index, not new construct signal — is the honest way to use GIST-like features, and the pure FFT/Gabor pipeline is deterministic. Two real problems keep it from clean VIABLE: (1) 'hand-set logistic over two hand-set discriminants' is an invented mapping unless the constants are calibrated and declared — V51 does this right, V50 as written does not; worse, the Oliva-Torralba openness discriminants were learned on outdoor scenes where openness = horizon dominance, and the sign/shape of those cues may not transfer indoors (a corridor is 'deep' but enclosed; a low wide room is open but horizonless). (2) It is algorithmically independent of the enclosure index but NOT photographically independent: wide-angle lenses and framing shift both estimators the same direction, so agreement inflates joint confidence more than it should. Self-capping at AMBER despite using no frozen model is the correct honesty call given unvalidated interior transfer.
V50.fixed_computation: Replace the hand-set logistic with the V51 pattern: calibrate the two discriminants on a small labeled interior set, declared as calibration constants, and verify on that set that the outdoor-derived discriminant directions actually hold indoors before shipping. Additionally, condition the DISAGREEMENT flag on estimated field of view (from vanishing-point geometry already in the stack) so lens-driven joint shifts are not read as independent agreement.

V51.reasons: The literature lineage is real and precisely cited: Torralba & Oliva 2002 (TPAMI) did predict mean depth from global spectral signatures, Ross & Oliva 2010 did predict human mean-depth judgments from global features, and Greene & Oliva 2009 supports glance-speed extraction. The design is the most epistemically disciplined in the batch: ordinal-only output, declared interior calibration set (avoiding the trap V50 falls into), and its product is explicitly an uncertainty flag on the frozen depth net rather than a confident number — exactly the single-point-of-failure guard this stack needs, since prospect currently rests entirely on one pretrained model. Known confounds are tolerable in the flag role: spectral scale statistics conflate scene size with texture/clutter (a close-up of a textured wall vs. a smooth atrium), and lens FOV shifts the radial spectrum; but a false disagreement flag costs only a widened uncertainty band, never a fabricated score, which is the right failure asymmetry. AMBER ceiling matches reality.

V52.reasons: Computability failure at the core of its own stated purpose. The metric claims to distinguish graded size hierarchy from mono-scale repetition, but perspective projection alone destroys that distinction: a row of identical chairs, windows, or ceiling tiles receding in depth produces a smooth gradient of image-plane component sizes, which the Clauset MLE will happily fit as an inverse-power distribution. The detector will report 'graded hierarchy ~ Salingaros coherence' for exactly the mono-scale minimalism it exists to flag. Depth-correcting areas would ride the depth net (still AMBER) and still fails under occlusion and slanted-plane foreshortening. Second, 'Canny -> closing -> connected components' does not yield countable design elements — edge maps chain unrelated structure together and fragment textured surfaces, so what gets counted is edge-ink blobs whose census is threshold- and exposure-dependent, not architectural components. Third, with a declared area floor and pixel-resolution ceiling you get at best ~1.5–2 decades of size range and often <100 elements; Clauset-Shalizi-Newman fitting is famously unreliable in that regime, so the KS gate will mostly return UNKNOWN, and the metric's yield collapses. Construct evidence is honestly labeled theory-grade (Salingaros & West 1999 is real but illustrative, not empirical validation of a wellbeing/cognition outcome), so there is no evidence weight to justify carrying these computability defects.
V52.fixed_computation: The only defensible re-proposal: restrict to a single wall/facade plane, homography-rectify it via the existing vanishing-point machinery so element sizes are metrically comparable within the plane, and extract elements by morphological granulometry or luminance-blob analysis on the rectified plane rather than global edge connected-components. That kills the perspective confound and matches Salingaros-West's actual (facade-level) domain — but it becomes a different, narrower predicate that abstains on most interior photos, and it still caps at AMBER on theory-grade construct evidence.

V53.reasons: The literature is real and honestly graded (White et al. 2010 JEP is genuinely a preference/affect/perceived-restorativeness ratings study and is sold as such; Völker & Kistemann 2011 is a real blue-space review correctly labeled proxy/self-report-grade; Ulrich 1983's preferenda framing is accurate). The failure is the Tier-A detector, and specifically its load-bearing claim: the mirror-correlation reflection test is presented as REJECTING glossy floors, but a polished marble or sealed-concrete floor exhibits exactly the vertical mirror correlation that still water does — the test admits the most common indoor false positive rather than excluding it. Since interior water features have a very low base rate, even a decent detector drowns in false positives (base-rate problem): most 'present' outputs will be glossy floors, glass tables, and screens showing blue content. Real indoor water (fountains, aquaria) is meanwhile textured or vertical and fails the smooth/below-horizon template, so recall on true positives is also poor. As a photo-only predicate this cannot be defended; as a declared-extra-input predicate (water-feature/aquarium annotation, or landscape plan for exterior views through glazing) the construct and its literature premium are perfectly sound within this framework's rules.
V53.fixed_computation: Reclassify as a declared-extra-input predicate (water-feature map / facility annotation), with a residual high-precision Tier-A path only when ALL of chromatic blue-cyan + textural smoothness + below-horizon + reflection pass jointly (dropping the standalone 'or specular' branch, which is the glossy-floor doorway) — reporting PRESENT only at that strict conjunction and ABSTAIN otherwise, with the precision/recall asymmetry declared.

V54.reasons: The mechanism citations are real and the scoping (salience only, Sussman & Hollander flagged as non-peer-reviewed) is commendably honest — but honesty about the downstream link does not rescue the measurement itself, and that is where this dies. The pareidolia literature (Taubert 2017; Liu 2014; Palmer & Clifford 2020) used stimuli that HUMANS verified as face-like; nothing anywhere validates that a Haar frontal-face cascade's hits on architecture, or a hand-crafted 'two co-elevated dark components plus a third below' template, correspond to what humans experience as pareidolic. Haar cascades are tuned to photographic face statistics and on non-face imagery produce sparse, essentially random texture hits; the geometric test detects a superset (any symmetric triadic dark arrangement) most members of which trigger no face percept, and it substantially overlaps the existing horizontal-symmetry attribute at local scale. So the number produced is 'symmetric triadic configuration density', not 'pareidolia salience' — an unvalidated detector-construct gap that is precisely the class of quiet fabrication this panel exists to catch. There is also a domain mismatch: the construct is facade-centric while the pipeline scores single interior photographs, so yield would be near zero even if the detector worked. Making it defensible requires a human-rating validation study of the detector on architectural imagery, which is a research project, not a computation fix.
